action.message: Must complete Phase {N} before Phase {N+1}. Current: {current_phase}

rules.rationale: 9-phase SDLC ensures quality, design-first approach, prevents premature implementation

examples.scenario: User in Phase 3 wants to jump to Phase 8
examples.correct: Explain must complete Phases 4, 5, 6, 7 first. Offer to expedite if needed.
examples.incorrect: Move directly from Phase 3 to Phase 8

rules.description: Phase 1 SEED must include: concept, problem, opportunity, assumptions, constraints, success criteria

action.message: Phase 1 deliverable: seed.md with 7 required sections

rules.rationale: Seed document ensures clear problem definition before research

examples.scenario: User: 'Create new idea for CLI tool'
examples.correct: Generate seed.md with: 1.0 Concept, 2.0 Problem, 3.0 Opportunity, 4.0 Assumptions, 5.0 Constraints, 6.0 Success Criteria, 7.0 Initial Approach Ideas
examples.incorrect: Create incomplete seed with only concept section

rules.description: Phase 2 RESEARCH existing solutions before designing new one

action.target: Research existing solutions, alternatives, prior art
action.message: Phase 2: Research existing solutions before expanding design

rules.rationale: Avoid reinventing wheel, learn from existing solutions, understand trade-offs

examples.correct: Research: Cube.js, dbt, LookML, AWS Glue, existing semantic layer approaches
examples.incorrect: Skip research, immediately expand design in Phase 3

rules.description: Phase 6 DESIGN must produce: architecture diagram, component breakdown, data models, API contracts

action.message: Phase 6 deliverables: TECHNICAL_DESIGN.md with diagrams, components, data models

rules.rationale: Detailed design prevents implementation surprises, enables accurate estimation

examples.correct: Create: System diagram, component breakdown, database schema, API endpoint specs, error handling patterns
examples.incorrect: Skip to Phase 7 with only high-level architecture sketch

rules.description: Phase 7 TEST_DESIGN: Write test specifications before implementation

action.target: Test specifications, test cases, test data
action.message: Phase 7: Design tests first (what to test, expected behavior)

rules.rationale: Test-first mindset ensures testability, clear acceptance criteria

examples.correct: Write: Login test cases, session test cases, permission test cases, edge cases, test data fixtures
examples.incorrect: Skip Phase 7, write tests during Phase 8 implementation

rules.description: Never write production code outside Phase 8 (except spikes)

action.message: Cannot write production code in {current_phase}. Reach Phase 8 first.

rules.rationale: Ensures design, testing planned before coding. Prevents premature implementation.

examples.scenario: Phase 6, user asks to 'start coding the API'
examples.correct: Explain: 'Phase 6 is design. Complete design, then Phase 7 tests, then Phase 8 code.'

rules.description: Web/UI projects must complete Phase 8.5 VALIDATION (E2E tests, screenshots, QA)

action.message: Web/UI project: Phase 8 → 8.5 (E2E, screenshots, QA) → Phase 9

rules.rationale: UI projects need visual/interaction validation beyond unit tests

examples.correct: Move to Phase 8.5: Run Playwright E2E tests, capture screenshots, manual QA checklist
examples.incorrect: Skip directly to Phase 9 refinement without validation

rules.description: SPIKE exceptions limited to 3 days max, throwaway code only

action.target: 3-day max timeline, code not merged to main
action.message: Spike: Max 3 days, throwaway code, findings inform design

rules.rationale: Spikes are learning tools, not production shortcuts. Time-box prevents scope creep.

examples.correct: Approve 3-day spike, create spike branch, document findings, don't merge code
examples.incorrect: Allow indefinite spike, merge spike code to production

rules.description: DIVERGENT mode requires confirmation: depth, width, evaluation criteria

action.message: Divergent mode: Confirm depth (1-3), width (3-5), evaluation criteria

rules.rationale: Divergent generates many variants, user must confirm scope and effort

examples.correct: Confirm: depth=1, width=3, eval criteria (cost, performance, complexity). Show effort estimate.

rules.description: Major phase transitions (3→4, 5→6, 7→8) require user approval

action.message: Phase {N} complete. Deliverables: {list}. Move to Phase {N+1}? y/n

rules.rationale: User approval at gates ensures quality, prevents rushing

examples.correct: Summary: 'Phase 5 complete. Selected: FastAPI + React. Move to Phase 6 design? y/n'
examples.incorrect: Automatically move to Phase 6 without user acknowledgment

rules.description: Update .project file on every phase transition
rules.rationale: Accurate .project metadata enables phase detection, tracking
